conversations.jd_after_snapshot:
# AI Engineer

## About Us
WOGOM is a B2B Commerce and Retail Enablement Platform, empowering 6,000+ retailers and 450+ sellers across India. Our goal is to build a connected ecosystem where technology, capital, and commerce converge to help Indian retailers scale with confidence.

## Role Overview
We're seeking a skilled AI/ML professional with a strong foundation in machine learning and deep learning fundamentals, proficient in Python programming, and experienced in model deployment and real-world AI implementation. The ideal candidate will have a mindset of collaboration, with the ability to work with cross-functional teams to understand requirements and integrate AI models into production systems.

## Key Responsibilities
• Design, develop, and deploy AI/ML models for real business use cases, leveraging machine learning and deep learning fundamentals.
• Work with structured and unstructured data to train and optimize models, and integrate AI models into production systems and internal tools.
• Collaborate with product, engineering, and business teams to understand requirements and drive business outcomes.
• Conduct thorough analysis of existing data infrastructure and monitor model performance.

## Requirements
### Must-Have Skills
• Machine Learning & Deep Learning fundamentals
• Python programming
• Model deployment & real-world AI implementation
• Data analysis and interpretation
• Collaboration and communication

### Nice-to-Have Skills
• TensorFlow / PyTorch
• SQL & Data Analysis
• APIs & backend integration

## Success Metrics
• Conduct thorough analysis of existing data infrastructure within 30 days
• Integrate AI models into production systems within 90 days
• Achieve high code quality and drive business growth through AI-powered solutions within 6 months

## How to Apply
To apply, please submit your resume and a cover letter explaining why you're a strong fit for this position. We look forward to hearing from you and exploring how you can contribute to WOGOM's mission.
